data.title: Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Wins HK$964 Million Hong Kong Hygiene Services Tender
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Ltd. ( (HK:3718) ) is now available.

Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Limited announced that its 85%-owned Hong Kong subsidiary, Shiny Glory Services Limited, has won a major tender from the Leisure and Cultural Services Department to provide cleaning and supporting services to leisure venues across several Hong Kong districts. The three-year contracts, expected to commence on 1 May 2026, carry an estimated total value of HK$964.54 million and annual revenue of about HK$321.51 million, although final figures may vary as formal contracts have not yet been signed.

Under the new mandate, Shiny Glory Services will deliver cleansing, pest control, waste and recyclable collection, and other support services to parks, playgrounds, and leisure venues in Islands, Tsuen Wan, Tuen Mun, Yuen Long, and Kwai Tsing. The successful bid reinforces the group’s position in Hong Kong’s environmental hygiene market and signals a meaningful expansion of its services portfolio and revenue base, while the company cautions investors that actual contract value and income could change with market conditions.

More about Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Ltd.

Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Limited is an environmental services provider focused on environmental hygiene, hazardous waste treatment, and waste electrical and electronic equipment treatment. Through subsidiaries such as Shiny Glory Services Limited in Hong Kong, the group targets public-sector contracts for large-scale cleaning, waste management, and related support services across urban districts and public facilities.

Average Trading Volume: 1,153,760

An announcement from Sino-Ocean Service Holding Ltd. ( (HK:6677) ) is now available.

Sino-Ocean Service Holding Limited has postponed the despatch of its shareholder circular on a planned parking space asset acquisition, citing the need for additional time for bulk printing. The circular, which will detail the transaction terms and include independent recommendations and valuation information, is now expected to be sent to shareholders on 6 May 2026.

The company has scheduled an extraordinary general meeting for 21 May 2026 to seek shareholder approval for the parking space asset acquisition agreement and related transactions. To determine eligibility to attend and vote, Sino-Ocean Service will close its register of members from 18 to 21 May 2026, a procedural step that sets the timetable for shareholder participation in approving the deal.

More about Sino-Ocean Service Holding Ltd.

Sino-Ocean Service Holding Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the property services sector. The company focuses on managing residential and related assets, and is expanding into associated facilities such as parking space assets to deepen its service portfolio for property owners and users.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sunshine Oilsands ( (HK:2012) ) has provided an update.

Sunshine Oilsands has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to its planned acquisition of a 51% equity interest in a target company, which will be funded through the issuance of consideration shares under a specific mandate. The circular, originally expected by April 30, 2026, is now scheduled to be dispatched on or before May 29, 2026, as the company requires more time to finalize and incorporate necessary information, and it cautioned investors that completion of the transaction remains subject to conditions and may not proceed.

The circular is set to provide detailed information on the equity agreement and acquisition, the independent board committee’s recommendation, advice from an independent financial adviser, financial data on both Sunshine and the target group, and notice of a special general meeting. The delay underscores ongoing procedural and disclosure requirements tied to the deal and signals continued uncertainty for shareholders and potential investors regarding the timing and outcome of the proposed transaction.

More about Sunshine Oilsands

Sunshine Oilsands Ltd. is a Calgary-based public corporation listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, focused on developing its large portfolio of oil sands leases in Canada’s Athabasca region. The company holds interests in roughly one million acres of oil sands and petroleum and natural gas leases and is currently concentrating on milestone development of its West Ells project, which has an initial production target of 5,000 barrels per day.

Easou Technology Holdings Limited ( (HK:2550) ) has issued an update.

Easou Technology Holdings Limited has convened its annual general meeting for June 30, 2026, in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended December 31, 2025. The agenda also includes re-election of executive directors Wang Xi and Chen Jun, authorization for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor for 2026, and renewal of a general mandate allowing the board to issue additional shares within prescribed limits.

These proposed resolutions, if approved, will maintain continuity in Easou’s senior leadership and external auditing arrangements while preserving board flexibility to raise capital through share issuance. The general mandate to allot and deal with additional shares, including treasury shares and convertible securities, underscores the company’s intent to keep financing and capital-structure options open, which may influence future dilution levels and funding strategies for its technology-focused operations.

More about Easou Technology Holdings Limited

Easou Technology Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2550. The company operates in the technology sector, although the specific products or services are not detailed in the notice, and it maintains operational presence in Shenzhen, China, where its annual general meeting will be held.

data.title: Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Names Kang Jian as New Chairman in Leadership Reshuffle
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited ( (HK:0412) ) just unveiled an update.

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited announced a leadership reshuffle effective 30 April 2026, with executive director and board chairman Li Tianzhang stepping down from his roles, including chairmanships of the executive and nomination committees, citing work adjustment and without any reported disagreements with the board. The company has appointed seasoned financier and economist Kang Jian as executive director and chairman, transferring to him the leadership of the executive and nomination committees, a move that brings extensive banking, investment and corporate management experience from major Chinese financial institutions and affiliated Shandong Hi-Speed entities, which may support the group’s strategic development and governance continuity.

Mr. Kang’s background includes senior roles at Shandong High Speed Sichuan Industrial Development, Qilu Expressway, Weihai Bank and Shandong Hi-speed Company Limited, as well as long tenures at Ping An Bank, Industrial Bank and China Construction Bank’s Shandong Branch, underpinned by degrees in investment economic management and finance from Shandong University of Finance and Economics. The board expressed respect and gratitude to Mr. Li for his contributions, while the appointment of Mr. Kang signals a continuation of state-linked infrastructure and financial sector expertise at the helm, likely reassuring investors and stakeholders about management stability during the transition.

More about Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in infrastructure-related investment and holdings. The group is affiliated with the wider Shandong Hi-Speed network of companies involved in expressways, industrial development and related financial interests in mainland China, reflecting a focus on transport, industrial assets and associated financial services.

SIIC Environment Holdings ( (SG:BHK) ) has shared an update.

SIIC Environment Holdings has issued a supplemental announcement related to its previously disclosed acquisition of 100% equity interests in certain target companies. The company has prepared unaudited pro forma financial information to illustrate the potential impact of the acquisition on its financial position as at 31 December 2025 and its 2025 statement of comprehensive income, using accounting policies consistent with its latest annual report.

The group also clarified clerical errors in an earlier announcement regarding the presentation currency of net tangible assets, profit attributable to shareholders, net tangible assets per share, and earnings per share. These amounts should have been denominated in RMB instead of RMB’000, and the per-share figures in RMB instead of RMB cents, with the revised pro forma statement detailing adjusted cash, receivables, and concession assets following the acquisition and related consideration.

data.title: Baidu Sets June 2026 Annual General Meeting and Confirms Record Date
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Baidu, Inc. Class A ( (HK:9888) ) has issued an announcement.

Baidu, Inc. has called its annual general meeting of shareholders for 9:00 a.m. on June 5, 2026, at its Beijing campus, with no shareholder proposals scheduled for approval at the meeting. The company has set May 18, 2026, as the record date for determining which holders of its ordinary shares may attend, and has outlined transfer and registration procedures through its Hong Kong share registrar.

The company also reminded investors that its 2025 annual report, along with the AGM notice and related materials, is available free of charge on its investor relations website, the Hong Kong Stock Exchange website, and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website. The announcement reiterates Baidu’s weighted voting rights share structure and confirms the current composition of its board, led by chairman and chief executive officer Robin Yanhong Li and supported by four independent directors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:9888) stock is a Buy with a HK$177.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Baidu, Inc. Class A stock, see the HK:9888 Stock Forecast page.

More about Baidu, Inc. Class A

Baidu, Inc. is a leading Chinese technology company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and best known for its internet search, AI, and cloud-based services. Its share capital features a weighted voting rights structure with Class A and Class B ordinary shares, and its American depositary shares trade on Nasdaq under the symbol BIDU, while its ordinary shares are listed in Hong Kong.

Average Trading Volume: 11,670,725
